name: MacTutor
biography:
Ren脙漏 de Possel 's parents were Marthe Seignon and Raoul de Possel, who was an examining magistrate. Ren脙漏 attended the Institution M脙漏lizan and the Lyc脙漏e at Marseille before going to Paris to study at the 脙聣cole Normale Sup脙漏rieure. After completing his Agr脙漏g脙漏 de mathematique, de Possel continued to undertake research for his doctorate. He spent time studying at Munich, G脙露ttingen and Berlin which had a major effect on broadening his education. In 1931 he published a result on the conformal mapping of a simply connected domain in a G脙露ttingen journal. He introduced the notion of sets of maximum type in a paper published in 1932 , and this concept was also studied in his doctoral thesis which was published in 1933 .
His studies were helped financially by the award of a Rockefeller Fellowship in 1930 - 32 before he gained his first appointment as Ma脙庐tre de Conf脙漏rences at the Faculty of Science at Marseille in 1933 . He then held the same position in the Faculty of Science at Clermont-Ferrand from 1934 where he joined Szolem Mandelbrojt . They would go to Paris to attend the S脙漏minaire de math脙漏matiques in the Institut Henri-Poincar脙漏 every second Monday. This gave them the chance to visit bookstores and libraries as well as meet up with other friends which they had made during their studied in Paris. They met regularly for lunch with their friends at the Caf脙漏 Capoulade in boulevard Saint-Michel near the Luxembourg Gardens and it was here that he became one of the founder members of the Bourbaki group.
In the early stages of Bourbaki , de Possel was involved on a subcommittee which considered how they would present integration theory. His views on integration are to be found in the expository article Les th脙漏ories modernes de l'int脙漏gration 芒聯聣 ( Modern theories of integration ) which he published in 1946 and in which he sketched an abstract form of the Riemann - Stieltjes integral. On many issues de Possel and Andr脙漏 Weil were on opposite sides in the arguments. At the first Bourbaki congress in July 1935 de Possel was still an active member of the group and much involved with contributing but, largely due to differences with Weil , he dropped out of the project. De Possel married Yvonne Liberati on 12 August 1935 ; they had three children, Yann, Maya, and Daphn脙漏. In 1936 he published an important book on game theory, namely Sur la th脙漏orie math脙漏matique des jeux de hasard et de r脙漏flexion 芒聯聣 ( On the mathematical theory of gambling and reflection ) . He published an article in 1935 which was the first to present a general theory of differentiation in abstract measure spaces and in the following year he published another article in which he gave complete proofs.
De Possel was appointed as professor in the Faculty of Science at Bresan脙搂on, then in 1938 professor in the Faculty of Science at Clermont. In 1941 he was appointed to a chair of mathematics in Algiers. He held the chairs of rational mechanics, then differential and integral calculus. In 1951 he was appointed to the specially created chair of higher analysis at the Faculty of Science at Algiers. From 1959 he was professor of numerical analysis at the Faculty of Science in Paris. We look briefly at the mathematics papers which de Possel published during these year.
In 1939 he published Sur la repr脙漏sentation conforme d'un domaine 脙聽 connexion infinie sur un domaine 脙聽 fentes parall脙篓les 芒聯聣 ( On the conformal representation of a connection to infinite domain on a domain with parallel divisions ) in which he developed further the ideas on conformal mapping of a simply connected domain which he had published in 1931 . In the second half of the 1940 s he published a considerable number of papers. We mention Sur l'ind脙漏termination de la puissance d'un torseur r脙漏parti 芒聯聣 ( On the indeterminacy of the power of a distributed couple ) in which he gave proofs of some formulas of use in the mechanics of continuous media, where the differential elements are subjected to couples as well as forces per unit volume; Les principes math脙漏matiques de la m脙漏canique classique 芒聯聣 ( The mathematical principles of classical mechanics ) which was based in ideas due to Brelot; Sur la d脙漏finition d'un torseur r脙漏parti et sur l'脙漏valuation de sa puissance 芒聯聣 ( On the definition of a distributed couple and the evaluation of its power ) which examines when external forces on part of a body are equivalent to couples alone; Initiation 脙聽 la topologie 芒聯聣 ( Introduction to topology ) resulting from work carried out in Portugal; Sur les syst脙篓mes d脙漏rivants et l'extension du th脙漏or脙篓me de Lebesgue relatif 脙聽 la d脙漏rivation d'une fonction 脙聽 variation born脙漏e 芒聯聣 ( On derived systems and the extension of the Lebesgue theorem about the derivation of a function of bounded variation ) extending the classical theorem for linear Lebesgue measure; and La notion physique d'脙漏nergie vis-脙聽-vis des d脙漏finitions du travail et de la force 芒聯聣 ( The physical concept of energy vis-脙聽-vis the working definitions and strength ) which considers the formulation of classical mechanics given by Brelot.
De Possel worked on computer science at the Institut Blaise Pascal of the National Centre for Scientific Research, in particular working on optical character recognition. His pioneering work in this field means that for many people this is the contribution for which he is best known. He became director of the Institut Blaise Pascal in 1962 and held this post until it was reorganised in 1968 . He continued to publish mathematics papers at during the years when he worked at the Institut Blaise Pascal: Deux lemmes sur les vari脙漏t脙漏s 脙聽 deux dimensions, et leur application au polygone fondamental d'une vari脙漏t脙漏 conforme prolongeable 芒聯聣 ( Two lemmas on varieties in two dimensions, and their application to the fundamental polygon of a  compliant extendable variety ) (1962) ; Sur les vari脙漏t脙漏s 脙聽 deux dimensions, les ensembles de capacit脙漏 maximale et le prolongement des vari脙漏t脙漏s conformes 芒聯聣 ( On the varieties in two dimensions, sets of maximum capacity and the extension of conformal varieties ) (1964) ; and Sur l'existence d'ensembles d'entiers qui interviennent en th脙漏orie ergodique 芒聯聣 ( On the existence of whole sets involved in ergodic theory ) (1966) . We mentioned that one of his early publications was a book on game theory and one of his last publications was also on game theory. In 1968 he published Graphes et jeux de "prise" ( nimspiele ) 芒聯聣 ( Graphs and games of 'taking' ( the game of Nim ) ) in which he:- ... announces the solution of several variants of the game of Nim with the following rules: Two players alternate in removing matches from p piles T 1 , . . . , T p T_{1},... ,T_{p} T 1 芒聙聥 , . . . , T p 芒聙聥 . The matches removed by a player in a turn must come from a single pile T n T_{n} T n 芒聙聥 and the number removed must belong to a set A n A_{n} A n 芒聙聥 of positive integers, fixed by the rules, but always containing 1 . The player to remove the last match wins. ( In another version, the player to remove the last match loses. ) Among his hobbies were watersports, and in particular he loved motor boats and underwater fishing.
